title: Cytogenetic Technologists
description: Analyze chromosomes or chromosome segments found in biological specimens, such as amniotic fluids, bone marrow, solid tumors, and blood to aid in the study, diagnosis, classification, or treatment of inherited or acquired genetic diseases. Conduct analyses through classical cytogenetic, fluorescent in situ hybridization (FISH) or array comparative genome hybridization (aCGH) techniques.

tasks.text: Arrange and attach chromosomes in numbered pairs on karyotype charts, using standard genetics laboratory practices and nomenclature, to identify normal or abnormal chromosomes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Count numbers of chromosomes and identify the structural abnormalities by viewing culture slides through microscopes, light microscopes, or photomicroscopes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Examine chromosomes found in biological specimens to detect abnormalities.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Apply prepared specimen and control to appropriate grid, run instrumentation, and produce analyzable results.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Select appropriate culturing system or procedure based on specimen type and reason for referral.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Analyze chromosomes found in biological specimens to aid diagnoses and treatments for genetic diseases such as congenital disabilities, fertility problems, and hematological disorders.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Harvest cell cultures using substances such as mitotic arrestants, cell releasing agents, and cell fixatives.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Summarize test results and report to appropriate authorities.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare biological specimens such as amniotic fluids, bone marrow, tumors, chorionic villi, and blood, for chromosome examinations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Select or prepare specimens and media for cell cultures using aseptic techniques, knowledge of medium components, or cell nutritional requirements.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Input details of specimen processing, analysis, and technical issues into logs or laboratory information systems (LIS).
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare slides of cell cultures following standard procedures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Input details of specimens into logs or computer systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Select appropriate methods of preparation and storage of media to maintain potential of hydrogen (pH), sterility, or ability to support growth.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop, implement, and monitor quality control and quality assurance programs to ensure accurate and precise test performance and reports.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Stain slides to make chromosomes visible for microscopy.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Describe chromosome, FISH and aCGH analysis results in International System of Cytogenetic Nomenclature (ISCN) language.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Evaluate appropriateness of received specimens for requested tests.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Create chromosome images using computer imaging systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Recognize and report abnormalities in the color, size, shape, composition, or pattern of cells.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Determine optimal time sequences and methods for manual or robotic cell harvests.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Communicate to responsible parties unacceptable specimens and suggest remediation for future submissions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Select banding methods to permit identification of chromosome pairs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Maintain laboratory equipment such as photomicroscopes, inverted microscopes, and standard darkroom equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Identify appropriate methods of specimen collection, preservation, or transport.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Archive case documentation and study materials as required by regulations and laws.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Supervise subordinate laboratory staff.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop and implement training programs for trainees, medical students, resident physicians or post-doctoral fellows.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Communicate test results or technical information to patients, physicians, family members, or researchers.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Extract, measure, dilute as appropriate, label, and prepare DNA for array analysis.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
